what_it_does: Defines a React modal component that subscribes to a global type-draft prompt, lets the user review and edit the draft's name, id, layer, family, description, and variables, then registers the draft through a global registry API.
observations: Uses React state for the active draft and a busy flag.
Subscribes to window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT in an effect and stores incoming drafts locally.
Returns null until a draft exists, making the modal conditionally present.
Clicking the backdrop or Cancel clears the draft and closes the modal.
Edits are applied with shallow patch helpers for the draft and variable rows.
Variables can be added, removed, and edited inline.
Save validates only draft name and id before calling window.CV_REGISTRY.register.
Layer options are read from window.CV_REGISTRY.LAYERS and window.CV_REGISTRY.LAYER_INFO.
The component is exported by assigning it to window.SaveAsTypeModal.
standouts: Acts as a review gate between an extracted draft and registry registration.
Keeps the draft editable in-place rather than immediately committing the promoted result.
Exposes variable editing as part of the type save workflow.
conventions: Uses globally scoped React aliases instead of imports.
Uses global window APIs for prompt subscription, registry access, toast messages, and component export.
Uses file-local helper functions nested inside the component.
Uses inline styles alongside class names.
Uses controlled form inputs for draft fields.
concerns: Assumes window.CV_REGISTRY, its LAYERS and LAYER_INFO members, and ViShape are available when the modal renders.
The register call has no local catch path, so registration errors are not surfaced through this component.
Variable rows use array indexes as React keys, so row identity can shift after removal.
Validation covers only name and id, not layer, family, variable key format, or duplicate variable keys.
The effect returns the result of optional subscribe directly, so cleanup behavior depends on the global subscription API returning a valid cleanup function.
notes: A small global React modal that turns an extracted type draft into a reviewed registry entry.
questions: What exact draft shape does window.CV_REGISTRY.register require?
Should variable keys be validated or deduplicated before saving?
What should happen when registration fails?
Can draft.layer be absent, and if so what default should the select use?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it wraps a promoted instance draft in a review/edit modal before registering it.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make AI- or tool-extracted type drafts inspectable and adjustable before they become saved registry types.
novelty: The modal combines type metadata editing with variable parameter editing in a transient global prompt flow.
contribution: Provides a file-local pattern for turning a generated or promoted design-system type draft into a user-reviewed registry entry with editable metadata and template variables.
summary_for_embedding: React modal component for reviewing, editing, and registering a draft registry Type with metadata fields and variable parameters.
intention_for_embedding: This was for adding a quick human confirmation and cleanup step between type extraction and registry persistence.

symbols.does: Renders and controls the save-as-type review modal.
symbols.description: This component listens for incoming type drafts through a global prompt subscription, stores the current draft in state, and renders a modal form when a draft exists. It provides controlled inputs for type metadata and variables, closes by clearing the draft, and exposes the component globally as window.SaveAsTypeModal.

symbols.does: Merges a partial object into the current draft state.
symbols.description: This helper updates top-level draft fields such as name, id, layer, family, and description. It uses a shallow merge against the existing draft object.

symbols.does: Merges a partial object into one variable at a given index.
symbols.description: This helper updates a single variable row by mapping over the draft variables array and replacing only the matching index with a shallowly merged object. If variables are absent, it treats them as an empty array.

symbols.does: Removes one variable from the draft by index.
symbols.description: This helper filters the draft variables array and excludes the row whose index matches the requested removal index. It preserves all other draft fields through a shallow object copy.

symbols.does: Appends a new default text variable to the draft.
symbols.description: This helper adds a variable object with a generated key, a New label, an empty default value, and text kind. The generated key is based on the current variable count plus one.

symbols.does: Validates required draft fields and registers the draft.
symbols.description: This function checks that the draft has a non-empty name and id, shows toast messages for missing values, and sets the busy flag while registration runs. It calls window.CV_REGISTRY.register with the draft, shows a success toast using the registered type name, then clears the draft.
